product.name: LaunchPack Web Studio
product.tagline: An async website launch system for productized client work.

hero.headline: A quiet operating system for launching small-business sites.
hero.subhead: This repo combines a package site, intake desk, template shelf, and handover kit into one repeatable flow.

summary: LaunchPack Web Studio is a working system for selling and delivering fixed-scope websites through an async process. The public side explains the offers, the intake side captures the brief, and the generator side turns that brief into a prepared client site and handover pack.
audience: Solo studios packaging website delivery into fixed offers
Micro-agencies that want an intake-first, async workflow
Builders who need repeatable starting points for client launches
coreProblems: Call-heavy intake slows down small website jobs.
Client builds need a repeatable starting structure instead of a blank canvas.
Deployment and handover notes often end up scattered across separate documents.

features.title: Public package desk
features.summary: The agency site presents three fixed offers, legal pages, contact routes, demo portfolio space, and template direction notes.

features.title: Structured intake
features.summary: The intake flow records package choice, services, deadlines, preferred template, and upload assets, then confirms by email.

features.title: Template shelf
features.summary: Three client-site starters share a typed content schema: Clean Modern, Bold Tradie, and Boutique Service.

features.title: Delivery pack output
features.summary: Generation also prepares credentials notes, SEO checklists, a handover email stub, and a site URL placeholder.

workflows.title: Offer and intake
workflows.summary: The public site sends clients into a structured intake rather than a booking funnel.

workflows.title: Review and status
workflows.summary: Submissions land in a dashboard with searchable records and status tags such as NEW, IN_PROGRESS, and DELIVERED.

workflows.title: Generate the build
workflows.summary: A submission can seed a client folder with typed content, a chosen template, copied assets, and a ready-to-edit starter.

workflows.title: Ship with notes
workflows.summary: The repo includes deployment notes, invoice helpers, credentials templates, SEO checklists, and handover email copy.

proofPoints.label: Offer catalog
proofPoints.note: Landing Page in 48 hours, Business Website Launch Pack, and Website Care Plan are defined in shared config and agency-site copy.

proofPoints.label: Template shelf
proofPoints.note: Clean Modern, Bold Tradie, and Boutique Service are included as template folders and shared constants.

proofPoints.label: Working surfaces
proofPoints.note: A public agency site sits alongside an internal intake dashboard.

proofPoints.label: Static fallback
proofPoints.value: GitHub Pages path for Offer A
proofPoints.note: Deployment notes explicitly describe a static export route for simpler single-page builds.

faq.question: Is this a brochure site or an actual workflow tool?
faq.answer: It is both. The repo contains a public package site and an internal dashboard that stores submissions, tracks status, and generates client-site folders.

faq.question: Does the workflow depend on calls?
faq.answer: No. The repo copy is explicit that intake and follow-up happen through forms and email rather than phone or headset work.

faq.question: Can it ship static sites?
faq.answer: Yes, within limits. The deployment notes call out a GitHub Pages fallback for Offer A style static exports, while the fuller workflow uses server-side features.

faq.question: Are real screenshots available?
faq.answer: Not in the public-safe materials used for this export. The visual section uses diagrams derived from repo structure instead.

screenshots.alt: Diagram showing the offer, intake, build, and handover flow.
screenshots.caption: The repo is organized around a quiet dispatch cycle: offer, intake, build, handover.

screenshots.alt: Diagram showing three template directions for generated client sites.
screenshots.caption: Three template directions give client builds a controlled starting point.

catalog.title: LaunchPack Web Studio
catalog.blurb: An async launch desk for productized website work, with intake, templates, and handover in one repo.

repoArchetypeReasoning: The README describes a monorepo with a public agency site, an internal intake dashboard, and client site templates.
The dashboard stores submissions, manages statuses, and generates client-site folders, which makes the repo more than a brochure site.
Because one surface is internal while another is public, the safest classification is an internal utility with a public-safe surface.
assumptions: Support and privacy URLs are placeholders for a future central site import.
Primary and secondary CTAs use internal anchors because no public live product URL is evidenced in the repo.
No public screenshots were committed, so repo-derived diagrams were created instead.
missingButUsefulAssets: Real screenshots of the agency site on desktop and mobile
A public-safe screenshot of the intake dashboard
A final brand mark or wordmark for LaunchPack Web Studio
One polished generated-client-site screenshot for the template system
